english: pa-més (pha mes) the grandfather by the father's, ma-més (ma mes) by the mother's side Cs; yaṅ-més (yang mes) great-grandfather Glr.; źe- (zhe-) or yži-més (yzhi mes) Sch. great-great-grandfather; mes-dbón (mes dbon) grandfather and grandchildren, resp., e.g. rgyál-po mes-dbón (rgyal po mes dbon) the kings from one generation to another, the royal ancestors Glr.; mes-rábs (mes rabs) id. Sch.; *me-mé* (*me me*), reverential name given to men of a more advanced age W. also C.

I num. figure: 136. II. woman, female, opp. to p'o (pho), = bud-méd (bud med): mo na-re (mo na re) the woman said Glr., Mil.; of animals: female. - *mo-kyáṅ* (*mo kyang*) W. virgin. - mo-gós (mo gos) woman's gown, petticoat. - mo-brgyúd (mo brgyud) female line of descent. - mo-bí (mo bi) female calf. - mo-byís (mo byis) Mil., mo-dbyís (mo dbyis) (*mo-yí* (*mo yi*)) C. girl, female child. - mo-btsún (mo btsun) nun Glr. - mo-mtsán (mo mtshan), moi dbáṅ-po (moi dbang po) female genitals. - mo-ráṅ-(mo) (mo rang-(mo)) 1. single, unmarried woman, so perh. in the passage, gdoṅ ṅán-gyi k'yó-bas mo-ráṅ skyíd (gdong ngan gyi khyo bas mo rang skyid) happier is a single woman than one with a husband of a bad face; more frq., the word implies 2. a poor, destitute female, one who did not get a husband W. 3. she, herself C., Lew. - mo-rí (mo ri), mo-ré (mo re) a female kid. - mo-rígs (mo rigs) female sex. Cs. - mo-lús (mo lus) the female body Sch. - mo-gśám (mo gsham) a barren female, hence mo-gśám-gyi bu (mo gsham gyi bu) a nonsense, an incongruity. III. lot, mo 'débs-pa (mo 'debs pa) to cast the lot, always a religious ceremony performed by Lamas (cf. rgyan (rgyan) and rtags-ríl (rtags ril)), which however does not preclude the possibility of an imposture; mó-pa (mo pa) one dealing with these practices, a soothsayer, mó-pa 'dre mt'óṅ-ba (mo pa 'dre mthong ba) a soothsayer that pretends to have seen a ghost; mó-mk'an (mo mkhan) Cs., mó-rtsis-pa (mo rtsis pa) Glr. id. (the latter expression in the respective passage = court-astrologer); mo-ma (mo ma) the feminine of it Cs., which however is at variance with Mil., who in several places has bla-ma mk'as-pai mo-ma (bla ma mkhas pai mo ma). IV. affix, so-called article, corresponding to the masc. terminations po (po) and pa (pa), and denoting the fem. gender of persons, bú-mo (bu mo) daughter, bód-mo (bod mo) a Tibetan woman.
dark (coloured) Cs.; móg-ro (mog ro) of horses, yellowish-brown Glr.
1. Cs. = móg-pa (mog pa). - 2. meat-pie, meat-balls in a cover of paste.
Lt. n. of a plant; in Lh. mog-śa-ras (mog sha ras) is a large species of Ferula or Dorema, of a yellow flower and a fetid smell.
W. for lóṅ-mo (long mo) knuckle, anklebone.

moment, occurring only in the following combinations: láṅ-bai mod (lang bai mod) (de-nyíd)-la ((de nyid)-la) at the very moment of rising Pth., Mil., dei mód-la (dei mod la) the moment after Glr.; gen. mód-la (mod la) instantly, immediately, mód-la dráṅs-so (mod la drangs so) Glr. he immediately pulled it out; k'ra yaṅ mód-la pyin-te (khra yang mod la phyin te) Dzl. immediately after there came also the hawk; dé-nas mód-la (de nas mod la) id. Dzl.

(cognate to mád-pa (mad pa)?) an emphatic word for to be, 1. as an augmentative of yin (yin), sometimes superadded to this word; occasionally untranslatable, sometimes = indeed, to be sure, źes smras mód-kyi (zhes smras mod kyi) Dzl. though indeed you may say so; dpag-tu-méd mód-kyi (dpag tu med mod kyi) though indeed it is immeasurable Dzl.; gsa dé-ka ṅa yin mod (gsa' de ka nga yin mod) Mil. the snow-leopard indeed was I myself; di ma yin mod 'on-kyaṅ ('di ma yin mod 'on kyang) ... to be sure, it is not this one, yet... Tar.; gró-ba yin mod ('gro ba yin mod) (although not invited) yet after all you must go. - 2. as augmentative of yod (yod), signifying abundance, plenty B., C., W.; de mi byéd-na dgra mod (de mi byed na dgra mod) if you omit to do this, you will have plenty of enemies, nad mod (nad mod) plenty of diseases; sti-bstáṅ-gi krims śin-tu mód-kyi (sti bstang gi khrims shin tu mod kyi) although they abounded in compliments; mód-pa (mod pa) having an abundance, loṅs-spyód mód-par gyur (longs spyod mod par gyur) he becomes the owner of great wealth Dzl.; śiṅ-tog mód- (shing tog mod-)
